title: Option to deactivate pod-name prefix in podman play kube
body:
### Feature request description

When running podman play kube the resulting container names are prefixed with the pod name. This may result in very long container names. While developing I would like to use rather short container names.

### Suggest potential solution

Add an option when running podman play kube to deactivate this behavior and use the actual container names defined in the yaml file or define an option to set a custom prefix which may be empty.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

The alternative would be to run the pod creation and container create commands manually but that means a lot more custom work in my build pipeline.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: Sure, seems reasonable to have an option for this. I wouldn't want it to be default, though.
Can I work on this please?
@arsenalzp would you still be interested in working on this? WE are in favor as such.

Hello,
Yes, I can, however, is this feature really needed?
I would consider it a nice-to-have - not essential, but we'd certainly merge it if it was done.
OK, let me work on it.

Hello @mheon 
Do you have any details or objection on my proposal from previous message last week?

Sorry for the delay.

I don't think we really need to add a character limit - having unusually long names shouldn't be all that common, as it requires a long pod name and container name.

> Sorry for the delay.
> 
> I don't think we really need to add a character limit - having unusually long names shouldn't be all that common, as it requires a long pod name and container name.

So can we close this issue?
Eh, I think there is some value in this for making `kube play` containers easier to to deal with, so I'd prefer not to close - but I agree that this is low impact/low priority

> Eh, I think there is some value in this for making `kube play` containers easier to to deal with, so I'd prefer not to close - but I agree that this is low impact/low priority

What would your propose/solution regarding this issue?

Hello. First of all thanks for all your efforts you put in so far.
I am not quite sure where this discussion went regarding complexity. Maybe I didn't articulate the requirement that well.
So here in more detail what I meant...

Let's say I create a pod named "dev-pod" and a container named "nginx":
`podman pod create --name dev-pod`
`podman create --name nginx --pod dev-pod docker.io/library/nginx:latest`


The container is named "nginx" and the pod "dev-pod" - as expected
`podman ps -ap`   
`3477d990b483   7 seconds ago   Created   16b9062caebb-infra    16b9062caebb  dev-pod`
`08f40a3cf412  docker.io/library/nginx:latest   nginx -g daemon o...  2 seconds ago   Created   80/tcp   nginx   16b9062caebb  dev-pod`


Now I create a yaml file from it:
`podman generate kube dev-pod -f dev-pod.yaml`
The yaml looks like this - as can be seen the name of the container "nginx" is also preserved here:

apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  annotations:
    io.kubernetes.cri-o.SandboxID/nginx: 363d310237d5359125ea1d651851c1054dbf0a7a463b44b62a78506fc527436e
  creationTimestamp: "2025-09-10T05:55:04Z"
  labels:
    app: dev-pod
  name: dev-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - args:
    - nginx
    - -g
    - daemon off;
    image: docker.io/library/nginx:latest
    name: nginx


Now lets delete the pod and recreate it:
`podman pod rm dev-pod`
`podman play kube dev-pod.yaml`

Now when I take a look at the container it has the name "dev-pod-nginx" instead of "nginx":
`podman ps -ap`
`a140cc4adca0   6 seconds ago   Up 4 seconds   c80eab0e2409-infra    c80eab0e2409  dev-pod`
`b89cf33c3c7e  docker.io/library/nginx:latest   nginx -g daemon o...  4 seconds ago   Up 4 seconds   80/tcp                                                                  dev-pod-nginx         c80eab0e2409  dev-pod`

So it would be nice to have some option in play kube to preserve the container name of the yaml like:

`podman play kube --no-prefixes dev-pod.yaml`

I think being able to just "deactivate" the default behavior of "podman play kube" adding prefixes with something like "--no-prefixes" is sufficient. This way one can have the preserved container names of the yaml with this option enabled. The default case still makes sense when you have multiple pods and need to distinguish. For me that would be enough - i don't see the need to set arbitrary prefixes
@arsenalzp Are you still intending to work on this?

Since it wasn't linked, there was some prior discussion (mostly restating what's in this thread) here: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16544

It hasn't been mentioned but I would also love an option for controlling pod naming, so that if I declare `metadata.name` in a Deployment the pod has exactly that name and not `my-name-pod` with the `-pod` suffix added.
